questions.stem_en: When renewing your driver's license, what is the eLearning alternative to the knowledge test?
questions.stem_zhHant: 續期駕照時，eLearning在線學習作為筆試替代方案是什麼形式？

options.text_en: A timed online exam you must pass
options.text_zhHant: 一個必須通過的限時在線考試

options.text_en: A no-fail, self-paced interactive course with quizzes after each module
options.text_zhHant: 一個不會不及格的、自定節奏的互動課程，每個模塊後有小測驗

options.text_en: A video lecture you must watch in a DMV office
options.text_zhHant: 必須在DMV辦公室觀看的視頻講座

options.text_en: A phone call with a DMV representative

questions.explanation_en: The eLearning option is a no-fail, self-paced interactive modulated course with quizzes after each module that can be taken on a computer, laptop, tablet, or mobile device.
questions.explanation_zhHant: eLearning選項是一個不會不及格的、自定節奏的互動模塊化課程，每個模塊後有小測驗，可以在電腦、筆記本、平板或手機上完成。

questions.stem_en: Beginning May 2025, what do you need a REAL ID compliant license or ID card for?
questions.stem_zhHant: 從2025年5月起，哪些情況需要符合REAL ID標準的駕照或身份證？

options.text_en: Driving on California highways
options.text_zhHant: 在加州高速公路上駕駛

options.text_en: Boarding domestic flights, entering military bases, and entering most federal facilities
options.text_zhHant: 乘坐國內航班、進入軍事基地和大多數聯邦設施

options.text_en: Purchasing a vehicle at a dealership
Registering to vote

questions.explanation_en: Beginning May 2025, a REAL ID compliant license or ID card is required to board domestic flights, enter military bases, or enter most federal facilities.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 從2025年5月起，乘坐國內航班、進入軍事基地或大多數聯邦設施需要符合REAL ID標準的駕照或身份證。

questions.stem_en: Which of the following is NOT allowed during a DMV knowledge test?
questions.stem_zhHant: 以下哪項在DMV筆試期間是不允許的？

options.text_en: Wearing glasses
Using a cell phone
Taking the test on a computer
Asking the examiner to clarify a question

questions.explanation_en: You are not allowed to use any testing aids during knowledge tests, such as a California Driver's Handbook or cell phone.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 筆試期間不允許使用任何輔助工具，如加州駕駛員手冊或手機。

questions.stem_en: On which devices can the eLearning renewal course be completed?
questions.stem_zhHant: eLearning續期課程可以在哪些設備上完成？

options.text_en: Only on a desktop computer at a DMV office
options.text_zhHant: 僅在DMV辦公室的台式電腦上

options.text_en: Computer, laptop, tablet, or mobile device
options.text_zhHant: 電腦、筆記本電腦、平板電腦或手機

options.text_en: Only on a tablet provided by DMV
options.text_zhHant: 僅在DMV提供的平板電腦上

options.text_en: Only on a laptop or desktop computer
options.text_zhHant: 僅在筆記本或台式電腦上

questions.explanation_en: The eLearning course can be taken on a computer, laptop, tablet, or mobile device.
questions.explanation_zhHant: eLearning課程可以在電腦、筆記本電腦、平板電腦或手機上完成。

questions.stem_en: What happens if a minor fails the knowledge test?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果未成年人筆試不及格會怎樣？

options.text_en: They can retake it immediately
They must wait 7 days before retaking it
They must wait 14 days before retaking it
They must wait 30 days before retaking it

questions.explanation_en: Minors must wait seven days to retake a failed knowledge test, not including the day of the failure.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 未成年人必須等待七天才能重考失敗的筆試，不包括失敗當天。

questions.stem_en: What must a law enforcement officer do before beginning any questioning during a traffic stop?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在交通攔停期間，執法人員在開始任何詢問之前必須做什麼？

options.text_en: Read you your Miranda rights
State the reason for the traffic stop
Call for backup
Ask for your immigration status

questions.explanation_en: Law enforcement officers must state the reason for a traffic or pedestrian stop before beginning any questioning related to a criminal investigation or traffic violation.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 執法人員在開始任何與刑事調查或交通違規相關的詢問之前，必須說明交通或行人攔停的原因。

questions.stem_en: In California, who is allowed to ask about your immigration status during a traffic stop?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在加州，誰可以在交通攔停時詢問您的移民身份？

options.text_en: Any law enforcement officer
Only state police officers
Only federal law enforcement officers
No one during a traffic stop
options.text_zhHant: 交通攔停期間任何人都不可以

questions.explanation_en: In California, only federal law enforcement officers can ask about your immigration status. State and local officers are prohibited from asking.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在加州，只有聯邦執法人員可以詢問您的移民身份。州和地方執法人員被禁止詢問。

questions.stem_en: If an officer asks to search your vehicle and you do not consent, what should you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果執法人員要求搜查您的車輛而您不同意，您應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Physically block the officer from searching
options.text_zhHant: 身體阻擋執法人員搜查

options.text_en: Drive away immediately
Clearly state you do not give permission, but do not physically resist
options.text_zhHant: 明確聲明不給予許可，但不要進行身體抵抗

options.text_en: Ignore the officer

questions.explanation_en: If you do not want the officer to search your vehicle, clearly state that you do not give your permission, but do not physically resist or obstruct the officer.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果您不希望執法人員搜查車輛，請明確聲明不給予許可，但不要進行身體抵抗或阻礙。

questions.stem_en: What right do you have regarding recording a traffic stop?
questions.stem_zhHant: 關於錄制交通攔停過程，您有什麼權利？

options.text_en: You may not record any interaction with police
options.text_zhHant: 您不得錄制任何與警察的互動

options.text_en: You may record in public spaces as long as it does not interfere with the officer's duties
options.text_zhHant: 只要不干擾執法人員的職責，您可以在公共場所錄制

options.text_en: You may record only with the officer's explicit permission
options.text_zhHant: 只有在執法人員明確許可的情況下才能錄制

options.text_en: Only passengers may record a traffic stop
options.text_zhHant: 只有乘客可以錄制交通攔停

questions.explanation_en: The First Amendment generally protects the right to record interactions with law enforcement in public spaces, as long as the recording does not interfere with the officer's duties.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 第一修正案通常保護在公共場所錄制與執法人員互動的權利，只要錄制不干擾執法人員的職責。

questions.stem_en: During a traffic stop, an officer can legally require the driver and passengers to:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在交通攔停期間，執法人員可以合法要求駕駛員和乘客：

options.text_en: Answer all questions about their personal life
options.text_zhHant: 回答所有關於個人生活的問題

options.text_en: Exit or stay inside the vehicle
Provide their social security numbers
Unlock their cell phones

questions.explanation_en: During a traffic stop, an officer can legally require the driver and all passengers to exit or stay inside the vehicle.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在交通攔停期間，執法人員可以合法要求駕駛員和所有乘客下車或留在車內。

questions.stem_en: A child under 2 years old, under 40 pounds, and under 3 feet 4 inches tall must be secured in what type of seat?
questions.stem_zhHant: 2歲以下、體重不足40磅且身高不足3英尺4英寸的兒童必須使用什麼類型的座椅？

options.text_en: A front-facing car seat
A rear-facing child passenger restraint system
A booster seat
A regular seat belt

questions.explanation_en: Children who are under 2 years old, under 40 pounds, and under 3 feet 4 inches tall must be secured in a rear-facing child passenger restraint system.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 2歲以下、體重不足40磅且身高不足3英尺4英寸的兒童必須使用後向式兒童安全座椅。

questions.stem_en: Where can you mount your cell phone for hands-free use while driving?
questions.stem_zhHant: 駕駛時可以將手機安裝在哪裡進行免提使用？

options.text_en: Only on the windshield
Windshield, dashboard, or center console
options.text_zhHant: 擋風玻璃、儀表板或中控台

options.text_en: Anywhere in the vehicle
Only on the dashboard

questions.explanation_en: You may mount your cell phone on the windshield, dashboard, or center console in a position that does not block your view of the road.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 您可以將手機安裝在擋風玻璃、儀表板或中控台上，不得遮擋視線。

questions.stem_en: For a vehicle with four-wheel ABS, what should you do during a locked wheel skid?
questions.stem_zhHant: 對於配備四輪ABS的車輛，在車輪鎖死打滑時應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Pump the brakes rapidly
Apply firm pressure on the brake pedal
options.text_zhHant: 對剎車踏板施加穩定壓力

options.text_en: Remove your foot from the brake completely
Turn off the engine

questions.explanation_en: For vehicles with four-wheel ABS, apply firm pressure on the brake pedal during a locked wheel skid. The ABS system will prevent the wheels from locking.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 對於配備四輪ABS的車輛，在車輪鎖死打滑時應對剎車踏板施加穩定壓力。ABS系統會防止車輪鎖死。

questions.stem_en: At what age or height can a child use a regular seat belt instead of a child restraint system?
questions.stem_zhHant: 兒童在什麼年齡或身高可以使用普通安全帶代替兒童安全座椅？

options.text_en: 6 years old or 4 feet tall
8 years old or at least 4 feet 9 inches tall
10 years old or 5 feet tall
12 years old or 4 feet 6 inches tall

questions.explanation_en: Children who are 8 years old or older, or at least 4 feet 9 inches tall, may use a properly secured safety belt.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 8歲及以上或身高至少4英尺9英寸的兒童可以使用正確固定的安全帶。

questions.stem_en: How may you interact with a mounted cell phone while driving?
questions.stem_zhHant: 駕駛時可以如何操作已安裝的手機？

options.text_en: You may type text messages if the phone is mounted
options.text_zhHant: 如果手機已安裝，可以打字發短信

options.text_en: You may use the single swipe or touch feature only
options.text_zhHant: 只能使用單次滑動或點擊功能

options.text_en: You may hold it in one hand while driving
You may not interact with it at all while driving

questions.explanation_en: While driving, you may only use the single swipe or touch feature on a mounted cell phone.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 駕駛時只能使用已安裝手機的單次滑動或點擊功能。

questions.stem_en: What causes hydroplaning?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼導致水漂現象？

options.text_en: Driving too slowly in rain
Tires losing all contact with the road due to water
options.text_zhHant: 輪胎因水而與路面完全失去接觸

options.text_en: Using high-beam headlights
Driving with tire chains

questions.explanation_en: Hydroplaning occurs when a vehicle rides on water because the tires lose all contact with the road.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 水漂現象是指車輛因輪胎與路面完全失去接觸而在水面上行駛。

questions.stem_en: What should you do during a law enforcement traffic break?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在執法部門進行交通管制時您應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Speed up to pass the patrol vehicle
Stop immediately wherever you are
Turn on emergency flashers and slowly decrease speed to match the officer's speed
options.text_zhHant: 打開雙閃燈並逐漸減速至與警官相同的速度

options.text_en: Change lanes to get around the officer

questions.explanation_en: During a traffic break, turn on your emergency flashers and slowly decrease your speed to match the officer's speed. Do not drive past the patrol vehicle.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在交通管制期間，打開雙閃燈並逐漸減速至與警官相同的速度。不要超越巡邏車。

questions.stem_en: If your vehicle stalls on railroad tracks and a train is approaching, what should you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果您的車輛在鐵軌上熄火且火車正在接近，您應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Stay in the vehicle and try to restart it
options.text_zhHant: 留在車內嘗試重新啟動

options.text_en: Exit and run away from the tracks perpendicular to the train
options.text_zhHant: 下車沿垂直於火車的方向跑離鐵軌

options.text_en: Exit and run diagonally away from the tracks in the direction the train is coming from
options.text_zhHant: 下車沿火車駛來的方向斜向遠離鐵軌跑

options.text_en: Wave at the train to stop

questions.explanation_en: If a train is approaching, immediately exit and run diagonally away from the tracks in the direction the train is coming from, then call 911.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果火車正在接近，立即下車沿火車駛來的方向斜向遠離鐵軌跑，然後撥打911。

questions.stem_en: Which is NOT a danger of driving on flooded roads?
questions.stem_zhHant: 以下哪項不是在被洪水淹沒的道路上駕駛的危險？

options.text_en: Being swept off the road
The road collapsing
Improved tire traction
Electrocution from fallen power lines

questions.explanation_en: Flooded roads reduce traction, not improve it. Dangers include being swept off the road, debris, road collapse, vehicle malfunction, and electrocution.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 被洪水淹沒的道路會降低抓地力而非提升。危險包括被沖離道路、碎片、路面塌陷、車輛故障和觸電。

questions.stem_en: When driving in high winds, you should NOT:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在大風中駕駛時，您不應該：

options.text_en: Reduce your speed
Use cruise control
Maintain a firm hand position on the steering wheel
Be alert for road debris

questions.explanation_en: Do not use cruise control in high winds. You need to maintain maximum control of the accelerator if a gust occurs.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 大風中不要使用定速巡航。如果遇到陣風，您需要保持對油門的最大控制。

questions.stem_en: What services does the CHP Freeway Service Patrol (FSP) provide for free?
questions.stem_zhHant: CHP高速公路服務巡邏隊（FSP）免費提供什麼服務？

options.text_en: Towing to your home or private repair shop
options.text_zhHant: 拖車到您家或私人修理店

options.text_en: Gas, jump starts, radiator refills, flat tire changes, and towing to a CHP-approved location
options.text_zhHant: 提供汽油、搭電啟動、加注冷卻液、換輪胎以及拖車到CHP批準的地點

options.text_en: Full vehicle repairs on the roadside
Motorcycle towing and collision assistance
options.text_zhHant: 摩托車拖車和碰撞事故協助

questions.explanation_en: FSP provides free gas, jump starts, radiator refills, flat tire changes, and towing to a CHP-approved location. They do not tow to private locations or tow motorcycles.
questions.explanation_zhHant: FSP免費提供汽油、搭電啟動、加注冷卻液、換輪胎和拖車到CHP批準的地點。他們不拖車到私人地點，也不拖摩托車。

questions.stem_en: When passing a bicyclist, how much space must you give?
questions.stem_zhHant: 超越自行車騎行者時，必須留出多少空間？

options.text_en: At least 1 foot
At least 2 feet
At least 3 feet
At least 5 feet

questions.explanation_en: You must allow at least three feet of space between your vehicle and the bicyclist when passing.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 超越騎行者時，必須在車輛和騎行者之間保持至少3英尺的空間。

questions.stem_en: What do shared roadway bicycle markings (sharrows) indicate?
questions.stem_zhHant: 共享道路自行車標記（Sharrows）表示什麼？

options.text_en: Bicycles are not allowed in the lane
options.text_zhHant: 自行車不允許在該車道行駛

options.text_en: Drivers should speed up to pass bicyclists
options.text_zhHant: 駕駛員應加速超越自行車

options.text_en: Bicyclists can occupy the lane and should maintain a safe position
options.text_zhHant: 自行車騎行者可以占用該車道並應保持安全位置

options.text_en: The lane is for bicycles only
options.text_zhHant: 該車道僅供自行車使用

questions.explanation_en: Shared roadway bicycle markings alert drivers that bicyclists can occupy the lane and help bicyclists maintain a safe lane position.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 共享道路自行車標記提醒駕駛員自行車騎行者可以占用該車道，並幫助騎行者保持安全的車道位置。

questions.stem_en: What is a yield line?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼是讓行線？

options.text_en: A solid yellow line across the road
A line of solid white triangles pointing toward approaching vehicles
options.text_zhHant: 一排指向接近車輛的實心白色三角形

options.text_en: A broken white line at an intersection
A red painted curb

questions.explanation_en: A yield line is a line of solid white triangles that shows approaching vehicles where to yield or stop. The triangles point towards approaching vehicles.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 讓行線是一排實心白色三角形，向接近的車輛指示在哪裡讓行或停車。三角形尖端朝向接近的車輛。

questions.stem_en: If you cannot give a bicyclist 3 feet of clearance when passing, what should you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果超越自行車騎行者時無法保持3英尺的間距，您應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Pass quickly at your current speed
Honk your horn and pass
Do not pass until you can give 3 feet of clearance
options.text_zhHant: 在能給出3英尺間距之前不要超越

options.text_en: Move to the opposite lane regardless of oncoming traffic
options.text_zhHant: 不管對向來車都移到對向車道

questions.explanation_en: If you cannot give three feet of space, do not pass the cyclist until three feet of clearance can be given.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果無法保持3英尺的間距，在能給出3英尺空間之前不要超越騎行者。

questions.stem_en: Which type of bike lane is physically separated from motor vehicle traffic?
questions.stem_zhHant: 哪種類型的自行車道與機動車交通物理隔離？

options.text_en: Standard bike lane
Bike route
Separated bikeway (cycle track)
Bicycle boulevard

questions.explanation_en: A separated bikeway (also known as a cycle track or protected bike lane) is physically separated from motor vehicle traffic by flexible posts, grade separation, barriers, or on-street parking.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 分離式自行車道（也稱為Cycle Track或受保護自行車道）通過彈性柱、高度分離、隔離欄或路邊停車與機動車交通物理隔離。
